title: May 25–29 Weekly Market Report
report:
## Market Overview (May 25–29)

35 of 67 themes (52%) posted gains. The average theme change was +0.82%, down from +1.24% the previous week. Rising US long-term yields and dollar strength weighed on sentiment, but MLCC/Electronic Components surged dramatically.

The Bank of Japan's policy meeting (May 28–29) resulted in no rate change, easing concerns about near-term hikes and providing mild relief for growth stocks.

---

## Top Rising Themes

### 🥇 1st: MLCC/Electronic Components (+9.4%)

The week's standout theme. Murata Manufacturing (6981) surpassed ¥14T in market cap — a record high — drawing capital into the entire MLCC space. Taiyo Yuden (6976) reported FY2026 Q4 net profit up +535% YoY, adding fuel to the rally.

### 🥈 2nd: Semiconductor Equipment (+6.8%)

3rd consecutive week in the top 5. Tokyo Electron (8035) and Advantest (6857) order backlogs remain at elevated levels, underpinning confidence in sustained AI capex.

### 🥉 3rd: Defense & Aerospace (+5.2%)

3rd consecutive week in the top 5. Specific program names in the supplementary defense budget were reported, driving buying in Mitsubishi Heavy Industries (7011) and Kawasaki Heavy Industries (7012).

### 4th: AI Semiconductors (+4.9%) / 5th: Space & Satellite (+4.1%)

AI infrastructure and national security themes continue to attract capital. Government satellite constellation tender announcements boosted the space sector.

---

## Bottom Themes

Oil (-4.2%) and LNG (-3.8%) fell sharply as OPEC+ continued production increases and global demand expectations weakened. Banking & Finance (-1.8%) dipped after the BOJ held rates.

---

---

## Next Week (Jun 2–6) Watch Points

- US Non-Farm Payrolls (Jun 6): Strong data could reignite rate hike fears
- Murata Manufacturing analyst target price revisions
- Defense supplementary budget — specific program/amount announcements
